name: Logfire (Pydantic's OpenTelemetry Observability)
pricing_notes: SDK open source (MIT); Logfire platform is commercial (Pydantic team); free tier available

auth.notes: Logfire token for cloud platform; or use with any OTLP backend

performance.notes: Async export; minimal overhead on hot paths

agent_readiness.notes: Pydantic's observability SDK built on OpenTelemetry. logfire.configure(token=...). logfire.instrument_fastapi(), instrument_sqlalchemy(), instrument_httpx(), instrument_openai(). logfire.span('operation') context manager. Structured logging with logfire.info('msg', key=value). AI/LLM observability (traces for OpenAI calls). Beautiful Logfire platform UI with SQL query interface. Exports to any OTLP backend (Grafana, Honeycomb, Jaeger). Alternative to Langfuse/Arize for LLM observability.

gotchas: Platform lock-in if using Logfire cloud vs OTLP export
Cloud pricing can escalate at scale
LLM observability features less mature than Langfuse
best_when: Python/FastAPI projects wanting OpenTelemetry with less setup
Teams using Pydantic wanting first-party observability
Projects needing structured logging + tracing in one tool
avoid_when: Already committed to Datadog/New Relic (limited ROI)
LLM observability depth needed (Langfuse more specialized)
summary: Logfire is Pydantic's OpenTelemetry-based observability SDK. One-liner instrumentation for FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, httpx, OpenAI. Structured logging + distributed tracing. Beautiful SQL-queryable Logfire platform. OTLP export to any backend. LLM/AI trace visibility. Built by the Pydantic team with excellent Python ecosystem integration. Best for FastAPI/Pydantic projects wanting easy OpenTelemetry with minimal config and strong LLM observability.
